ingredients_string: Black Pepper:2 | Butternut Squash:1000 | Garlic:10 | Homemade Coconut Milk:100 | Nutmeg:1 | Olive Oil:20 | Pumpkin Seeds:30 | Sea Salt:10 | Vegetable Broth (Base):750 | White Onion:80
short_description: A silky velvet roasted pumpkin soup blended with light coconut cream and spices
instructions:
Caramelize the Aromatics & Squash
Preheat your oven to 200°C and line a large baking sheet with unbleached parchment paper.
Toss the uniform butternut squash cubes, chopped white onions, and smashed garlic cloves directly on the tray with extra virgin olive oil, a pinch of sea salt, and a crack of black pepper.
Spread them into a single layer so they roast rather than steam.
Bake for 25–30 minutes, tossing halfway through, until the squash edges turn deeply golden-brown and can be effortlessly pierced with a fork.
Build the Soup Base
Transfer the hot roasted squash, sweet caramelized onions, and garlic directly into a heavy-bottomed stainless steel stockpot.
Pour in 3 cups of hot Vegetable Broth (Base). Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium heat for 5 minutes, allowing the vegetable cells to absorb the broth flavors.
Emulsify and Puree
Remove the pot from the heat source. Use an immersion hand-blender directly in the pot (or carefully transfer the mixture in batches to a high-speed countertop blender) and process until the texture is completely smooth, velvety, and free of strings.
Place the pot back over low heat.
Infuse Fats and Warming Spices
Pour in 1 cup of creamy Coconut Milk, stirring constantly to smoothly integrate the healthy medium-chain fats.
Stir in the freshly grated Nutmeg and ground Cinnamon.
Taste the soup matrix and adjust the seasoning with an extra pinch of sea salt or black pepper if needed. Simmer gently on low for another 3 minutes just to heat through—do not let it hit a rolling boil once the coconut milk is added to avoid separation.
Plating and Garnish Layer
Ladle the velvety soup into warm bowls.
Drizzle a clean swirl of remaining coconut milk over the surface, and scatter the raw or flash-toasted Pumpkin Seeds across the center for a premium, textured crunch contrast.
